lessons.subject: Home Language
lessons.title: Grade 3 Listening and speaking — Core teaching
learning_objectives: Engage with listening and speaking tasks appropriate to the grade and language level.
Use vocabulary, comprehension strategies and conventions from the CAPS source document.
Produce oral, written or visual responses with increasing accuracy and confidence.
lessons.lesson_body:
In this lesson learners study listening and speaking using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Engage with listening and speaking tasks appropriate to the grade and language level. Key vocabulary: Listening, speaking, ENGLISH, LANGUAGE. Read the passage carefully, notice key words, and explain answers in full sentences. Grounding snippet: --- page 6 ---
ENGLISH HOME LANGUAGE GRADES R-3
1
CAPS
CONTENTS
seCtion 1: introduCtion to the CurriCulum and assessment PoliCy statements .. 3
1.1 Background ............................................................. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.
lessons.explanation:
In this lesson learners study listening and speaking using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Engage with listening and speaking tasks appropriate to the grade and language level. Key vocabulary: Listening, speaking, ENGLISH, LANGUAGE. Read the passage carefully, notice key words, and explain answers in full sentences. Grounding snippet: --- page 6 ---
ENGLISH HOME LANGUAGE GRADES R-3
1
CAPS
CONTENTS
seCtion 1: introduCtion to the CurriCulum and assessment PoliCy statements .. 3
1.1 Background ............................................................. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: Which sentence best states the main idea of the passage about listening and speaking?
step_by_step_solution: Read the passage once for meaning.
Underline the sentence that names the topic and the writer's main point.
Compare the options and reject answers that mention only one detail.
Select the option that covers the whole passage.
worked_examples.answer: The passage explains how learners use listening and speaking in reading and writing.

worked_examples.question: Replace the underlined word in 'The learners read a short passage about listening and speaking at school.' with a stronger verb.
step_by_step_solution: Identify the verb that can be replaced.
Choose a verb that keeps the same meaning but is more precise.
Read the new sentence aloud to check grammar.
Write the improved sentence.
worked_examples.answer: The learners analysed a short passage about listening and speaking at school.

practice_questions.question_text: Which sentence uses a comma correctly in a Grade 3 text about listening and speaking?

options.A: After reading the learners discussed listening and speaking
options.B: After reading, the learners discussed listening and speaking.
options.C: After, reading the learners discussed listening and speaking.
options.D: After reading the, learners discussed listening and speaking.

practice_questions.explanation: A comma follows the introductory phrase correctly.

practice_questions.question_text: Which word is the strongest verb for a report on listening and speaking?

options.A: did
options.B: went
options.C: analysed
options.D: got

practice_questions.explanation: Analysed is precise and academic.

practice_questions.question_text: Which option best summarises a paragraph about listening and speaking?

options.A: It lists every punctuation mark in the passage.
options.B: It only names the author of the passage.
options.C: It repeats one example sentence without a main idea.
options.D: It explains how listening and speaking helps learners read and write more accurately.

practice_questions.explanation: A summary states the main idea of the whole paragraph.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: After reading, the learners discussed listening and speaking.
analysed
It explains how listening and speaking helps learners read and write more accurately.

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for listening and speaking and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Reread the sentence aloud and check punctuation and verb choice.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 3 example about listening and speaking and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 3.HL.1.1 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect listening and speaking to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for listening and speaking. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: languages.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about listening and speaking without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports languages learning at home.

Key vocabulary: Listening, speaking, ENGLISH, LANGUAGE. Read the passage carefully, notice key words, and explain answers in full sentences. Grounding snippet: --- page 6 ---
ENGLISH HOME LANGUAGE GRADES R-3
1
CAPS
CONTENTS
seCtion 1: introduCtion to the CurriCulum and assessment PoliCy statements .. 3
1.1 Background ............................................................. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: Which sentence best states the main idea of the passage about listening and speaking?
step_by_step_solution: Read the passage once for meaning.
Underline the sentence that names the topic and the writer's main point.
Compare the options and reject answers that mention only one detail.
Select the option that covers the whole passage.
worked_examples.answer: The passage explains how learners use listening and speaking in reading and writing.

worked_examples.question: Replace the underlined word in 'The learners read a short passage about listening and speaking at school.' with a stronger verb.
step_by_step_solution: Identify the verb that can be replaced.
Choose a verb that keeps the same meaning but is more precise.
Read the new sentence aloud to check grammar.
Write the improved sentence.
worked_examples.answer: The learners analysed a short passage about listening and speaking at school.

practice_questions.question_text: Which sentence uses a comma correctly in a Grade 3 text about listening and speaking?

options.A: After reading the learners discussed listening and speaking
options.B: After, reading the learners discussed listening and speaking.
options.C: After reading the, learners discussed listening and speaking.
options.D: After reading, the learners discussed listening and speaking.

practice_questions.explanation: A comma follows the introductory phrase correctly.

practice_questions.question_text: Which word is the strongest verb for a report on listening and speaking?

options.A: analysed
options.B: did
options.C: went
options.D: got

practice_questions.explanation: Analysed is precise and academic.

practice_questions.question_text: Which option best summarises a paragraph about listening and speaking?

options.A: It lists every punctuation mark in the passage.
options.B: It explains how listening and speaking helps learners read and write more accurately.
options.C: It only names the author of the passage.
options.D: It repeats one example sentence without a main idea.

practice_questions.explanation: A summary states the main idea of the whole paragraph.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: After reading, the learners discussed listening and speaking.
analysed
It explains how listening and speaking helps learners read and write more accurately.

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for listening and speaking and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Reread the sentence aloud and check punctuation and verb choice.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 3 example about listening and speaking and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 3.HL.1.1 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect listening and speaking to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for listening and speaking. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: languages.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about listening and speaking without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports languages learning at home.

learning_objectives: Engage with language structures and conventions tasks appropriate to the grade and language level.
Use vocabulary, comprehension strategies and conventions from the CAPS source document.
Produce oral, written or visual responses with increasing accuracy and confidence.
lessons.lesson_body: This visual lesson helps learners see language structures and conventions with diagrams, models and labelled drawings. CAPS focus: Engage with language structures and conventions tasks appropriate to the grade and language level. Key vocabulary: Language, structures, conventions. Read the passage carefully, notice key words, and explain answers in full sentences. Grounding snippet: CAPS reference 3.HL.1.4 covers Language structures and conventions. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.
lessons.explanation: This visual lesson helps learners see language structures and conventions with diagrams, models and labelled drawings. CAPS focus: Engage with language structures and conventions tasks appropriate to the grade and language level. Key vocabulary: Language, structures, conventions. Read the passage carefully, notice key words, and explain answers in full sentences. Grounding snippet: CAPS reference 3.HL.1.4 covers Language structures and conventions. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: Which sentence best states the main idea of the passage about language structures and conventions?
step_by_step_solution: Read the passage once for meaning.
Underline the sentence that names the topic and the writer's main point.
Compare the options and reject answers that mention only one detail.
Select the option that covers the whole passage.
worked_examples.answer: The passage explains how learners use language structures and conventions in reading and writing.

worked_examples.question: Replace the underlined word in 'The learners read a short passage about language structures and conventions at school.' with a stronger verb.
step_by_step_solution: Identify the verb that can be replaced.
Choose a verb that keeps the same meaning but is more precise.
Read the new sentence aloud to check grammar.
Write the improved sentence.
worked_examples.answer: The learners analysed a short passage about language structures and conventions at school.

practice_questions.question_text: Which sentence uses a comma correctly in a Grade 3 text about language structures and conventions?

options.A: After reading the learners discussed language structures and conventions
options.B: After, reading the learners discussed language structures and conventions.
options.C: After reading, the learners discussed language structures and conventions.
options.D: After reading the, learners discussed language structures and conventions.

practice_questions.explanation: A comma follows the introductory phrase correctly.

practice_questions.question_text: Which word is the strongest verb for a report on language structures and conventions?

options.A: did
options.B: went
options.C: got
options.D: analysed

practice_questions.explanation: Analysed is precise and academic.

practice_questions.question_text: Which option best summarises a paragraph about language structures and conventions?

options.A: It explains how language structures and conventions helps learners read and write more accurately.
options.B: It lists every punctuation mark in the passage.
options.C: It only names the author of the passage.
options.D: It repeats one example sentence without a main idea.

practice_questions.explanation: A summary states the main idea of the whole paragraph.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: After reading, the learners discussed language structures and conventions.
analysed
It explains how language structures and conventions helps learners read and write more accurately.

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for language structures and conventions and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Reread the sentence aloud and check punctuation and verb choice.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 3 example about language structures and conventions and teach it to a partner using the visual approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 3.HL.1.4 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect language structures and conventions to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for language structures and conventions. Use the visual variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: languages.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about language structures and conventions without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports languages learning at home.

source_citations.citation_text: CAPS reference 3.HL.1.4 covers Language structures and conventions.

options.B: It only names the author of the passage.
options.C: It repeats one example sentence without a main idea.
options.D: It explains how language structures and conventions helps learners read and write more accurately.

practice_questions.explanation: A summary states the main idea of the whole paragraph.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: After reading, the learners discussed language structures and conventions.
analysed
It explains how language structures and conventions helps learners read and write more accurately.

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for language structures and conventions and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Reread the sentence aloud and check punctuation and verb choice.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 3 example about language structures and conventions and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 3.HL.3.4 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect language structures and conventions to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for language structures and conventions. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: languages.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about language structures and conventions without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports languages learning at home.

source_citations.citation_text: CAPS reference 3.HL.3.4 covers Language structures and conventions.

options.B: After reading the learners discussed reading and viewing
options.C: After, reading the learners discussed reading and viewing.
options.D: After reading the, learners discussed reading and viewing.

practice_questions.explanation: A comma follows the introductory phrase correctly.

practice_questions.question_text: Which word is the strongest verb for a report on reading and viewing?

options.A: did
options.B: analysed
options.C: went
options.D: got

practice_questions.explanation: Analysed is precise and academic.

practice_questions.question_text: Which option best summarises a paragraph about reading and viewing?

options.A: It lists every punctuation mark in the passage.
options.B: It only names the author of the passage.
options.C: It explains how reading and viewing helps learners read and write more accurately.
options.D: It repeats one example sentence without a main idea.

practice_questions.explanation: A summary states the main idea of the whole paragraph.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: After reading, the learners discussed reading and viewing.
analysed
It explains how reading and viewing helps learners read and write more accurately.

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for reading and viewing and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Reread the sentence aloud and check punctuation and verb choice.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 3 example about reading and viewing and teach it to a partner using the step by step approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 3.HL.4.2 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect reading and viewing to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for reading and viewing. Use the step by step variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: languages.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about reading and viewing without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports languages learning at home.

options.B: After reading, the learners discussed language structures and conventions.
options.C: After, reading the learners discussed language structures and conventions.
options.D: After reading the, learners discussed language structures and conventions.

practice_questions.explanation: A comma follows the introductory phrase correctly.

practice_questions.question_text: Which word is the strongest verb for a report on language structures and conventions?

options.A: did
options.B: went
options.C: analysed
options.D: got

practice_questions.explanation: Analysed is precise and academic.

practice_questions.question_text: Which option best summarises a paragraph about language structures and conventions?

options.A: It lists every punctuation mark in the passage.
options.B: It only names the author of the passage.
options.C: It repeats one example sentence without a main idea.
options.D: It explains how language structures and conventions helps learners read and write more accurately.

practice_questions.explanation: A summary states the main idea of the whole paragraph.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: After reading, the learners discussed language structures and conventions.
analysed
It explains how language structures and conventions helps learners read and write more accurately.

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for language structures and conventions and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Reread the sentence aloud and check punctuation and verb choice.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 3 example about language structures and conventions and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 3.HL.4.4 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect language structures and conventions to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for language structures and conventions. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: languages.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about language structures and conventions without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports languages learning at home.

step_by_step_solution: Read the passage once for meaning.
Underline the sentence that names the topic and the writer's main point.
Compare the options and reject answers that mention only one detail.
Select the option that covers the whole passage.
worked_examples.answer: The passage explains how learners use language structures and conventions in reading and writing.

worked_examples.question: Replace the underlined word in 'The learners read a short passage about language structures and conventions at school.' with a stronger verb.
step_by_step_solution: Identify the verb that can be replaced.
Choose a verb that keeps the same meaning but is more precise.
Read the new sentence aloud to check grammar.
Write the improved sentence.
worked_examples.answer: The learners analysed a short passage about language structures and conventions at school.

practice_questions.question_text: Which sentence uses a comma correctly in a Grade 3 text about language structures and conventions?

options.A: After reading, the learners discussed language structures and conventions.
options.B: After reading the learners discussed language structures and conventions
options.C: After, reading the learners discussed language structures and conventions.
options.D: After reading the, learners discussed language structures and conventions.

practice_questions.explanation: A comma follows the introductory phrase correctly.

practice_questions.question_text: Which word is the strongest verb for a report on language structures and conventions?

options.A: did
options.B: analysed
options.C: went
options.D: got

practice_questions.explanation: Analysed is precise and academic.

practice_questions.question_text: Which option best summarises a paragraph about language structures and conventions?

options.A: It lists every punctuation mark in the passage.
options.B: It only names the author of the passage.
options.C: It explains how language structures and conventions helps learners read and write more accurately.
options.D: It repeats one example sentence without a main idea.

practice_questions.explanation: A summary states the main idea of the whole paragraph.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: After reading, the learners discussed language structures and conventions.
analysed
It explains how language structures and conventions helps learners read and write more accurately.

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for language structures and conventions and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Reread the sentence aloud and check punctuation and verb choice.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 3 example about language structures and conventions and teach it to a partner using the visual approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 3.HL.4.4 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect language structures and conventions to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for language structures and conventions. Use the visual variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: languages.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about language structures and conventions without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports languages learning at home.

source_citations.citation_text: CAPS reference 3.HL.4.4 covers Language structures and conventions.
